VOL LANDSCAPE & ANALYTICS

The VIX at 27.5 sits in backwardation with VIX3M trading 190bp below, marking the steepest inversion in six weeks as dealers prepare for vol reset. Broad equity IV ranks have spiked to 90th percentile across SPY, EEM, and IWM while put skew reaches extreme 99th percentile levels. Credit spreads remain contained despite equity vol expansion, creating a cross-asset divergence that typically resolves within 48 hours.

Market Intelligence Brief
Generated 16:32 ET
ARM two-way volatility explosion dominates amid CPU market entry catalyst
KEY TAKEAWAYS
  • 1. ARM's CPU market expansion news drives extraordinary two-way flow — calls +941%, puts +551% — creating pure volatility play opportunity.
  • 2. Extreme regime divergence with bullish single-name flow (+0.77 P/C) against bearish index positioning (1.36 P/C) suggests institutional dispersion hedging.
  • 3. Negative $15.4B SPY gamma amplifies all directional moves ahead of tomorrow's Core PCE print.
NARRATIVE — divergent flow bearish macro

ARM's announcement of CPU market entry plans has triggered the most extreme two-way options flow seen this year, with call volume exploding +941% and put volume surging +551% as investors position for binary outcomes around the Nvidia datacenter challenge. The simultaneous surge in both directions signals pure volatility demand rather than directional conviction, with ARM's IV now in backwardation as near-term uncertainty pricing exceeds longer-dated expectations. TERN exhibits similar two-way flow patterns (+815% calls, +467% puts) following MRK's acquisition announcement, confirming deal completion risk as the primary driver rather than informed directional positioning. The most significant structural signal emerges from the extreme P/C ratio divergence — index puts heavy at 1.36 versus single-name calls heavy at 0.77, a 0.59x spread indicating sophisticated dispersion hedging. This positioning suggests institutional managers are buying index downside protection while simultaneously targeting individual stock upside, consistent with a view that market weakness would create stock-picking opportunities. The 0.37 implied correlation reading supports this thesis, sitting in the stock-specific regime where single-name volatility trades more efficiently than broad market hedges. Dealer positioning at negative $15.4B SPY gamma creates a dangerous amplification mechanism heading into tomorrow's Core PCE release, with the gamma flip at $665 sitting just 2% above current levels. Any Core PCE surprise that pushes SPX meaningfully higher could trigger explosive upside acceleration as dealers cover short gamma positions, while disappointment would see similar acceleration to the downside through the $650 put wall.

Layer 1: Sentiment -1Layer 2: GEX -$15.4BLayer 3: P/C divergence 0.59xLayer 8: ARM CPU newsLayer 9: Core PCE 1d

ATM IV = at-the-money implied vol  |  IV PCTILE = where today's IV ranks vs ≈90 trading days (green=cheap, red=expensive)  |  Spread = ATM IV − RV 20d  |  Rich: IV/RV >1.2×  |  Skew 90/110: P(90%) − C(110%)  |  SKEW PCTILE: vs ≈90d history  |  PUT/CALL V%: daily volume rank vs ≈90d history  |  vs SECTOR: ticker ATM IV / sector ETF ATM IV (±10% threshold)  |  Divergence: cross-signal flags (VOL HIGH/SKEW FLAT, CHEAP VOL/RICH SKEW, etc.)
